description: Standard plugging conditions derived from NM OCD Figures A-D

trigger.description: Well has perforated intervals in production zone

requirement.note: Cement plug must straddle all perforated intervals. Minimum 100 ft or 25 sacks, whichever is greater.

trigger.description: Well has casing set with cement shoe

requirement.note: Cement plug required at or near casing shoe to isolate open hole from cased hole.

trigger.description: Open hole interval exists below deepest casing shoe

requirement.note: Open hole below casing shoe must be plugged with minimum 100 ft cement or 25 sacks.

trigger.description: Well penetrates usable quality water zones

requirement.note: Cement plug must isolate deepest usable quality water zone. 50 ft minimum.

trigger.description: Intermediate formation zone requiring isolation between production and surface

requirement.note: Intermediate formations between production and surface zones must be isolated.

trigger.description: Well has multiple producing zones that must be individually isolated

requirement.note: Each producing zone must be isolated from adjacent zones with cement plugs.

trigger.description: All wells require a surface plug

requirement.note: Surface plug top must be within 50 ft of ground surface. Required for all abandoned wells.

trigger.description: Cased hole sections must not exceed maximum plug spacing

requirement.note: No interval in cased hole may exceed 3000 ft without a cement plug (NMAC 19.15.25).

trigger.description: Open hole sections must not exceed maximum plug spacing

requirement.note: No interval in open hole may exceed 2000 ft without a cement plug (NMAC 19.15.25).

trigger.description: All cement plugs require minimum WOC time

requirement.note: Minimum 4 hours WOC required before tagging or drilling out cement (NMAC 19.15.25).

trigger.description: Cement class must be appropriate for depth

requirement.note: Class A/C for depths < 6000 ft; Class G/H for depths >= 6000 ft.
